acceptance_targets: [ch13-craft-01] Reading snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:313-337 in order, each paragraph after Inez's break must introduce a new consequence carrier or escalate the damage; no adjacent paragraphs may simply restate public repetition, and the final paragraph must land on a sharper cost than "the name is everywhere."

[ch13-dialogue-01] In the revised cluster around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:31-45, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:91, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:251-253, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:293, no more than two speakers plus the narrator use the exact adjectives "clean" or "dirty"; the other beats must convey the same pressure through voice-specific language that reads as Junie, Inez, or Gideon rather than shared chapter shorthand.

[ch13-prose-01] Across the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:91, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:173, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:231, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:245, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:275, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:321, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:327, no more than two sentence-initial evaluative "That..." constructions remain, and each touched paragraph ends on a specific sensory or social consequence rather than narrator labeling.

findings.acceptance_test: Reading snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:313-337 in order, each paragraph after Inez's break must introduce a new consequence carrier or escalate the damage; no adjacent paragraphs may simply restate public repetition, and the final paragraph must land on a sharper cost than "the name is everywhere."
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:297 lands the personal break hard; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:313, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:321, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:327, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:337 then keep re-demonstrating that the name is spreading after the chapter has already proved it.
findings.problem: The chapter reaches its sharpest consequence when Inez refuses explanation and turns to emergency work, but the coda keeps circling the spread of "Celadon Crown" through several adjacent beats. That iterative proof weakens the hard shame-rhythm the scene has earned and lets the ending explain momentum instead of cutting on it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten the exit in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:313-337. Keep the strongest immediate domestic consequence beat and one citywide uptake beat, then cut or transform the remaining repetition so each surviving paragraph changes stakes rather than restating that the name has caught. If the chapter ends on a generalizing paragraph, make it carry a fresh cost not already shown in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:317-333.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised cluster around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:31-45, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:91, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:251-253, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:293, no more than two speakers plus the narrator use the exact adjectives "clean" or "dirty"; the other beats must convey the same pressure through voice-specific language that reads as Junie, Inez, or Gideon rather than shared chapter shorthand.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:31 gives Junie "keep your mouth dirty" / "Clean speech"; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:45 has Inez warn against a "clean line"; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:91 has Gideon demand a "clean" delivery; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:251-253 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:293 repeat the same clean/dirty vocabulary in Silas's interiority and narration.
findings.problem: The scene's clean-versus-dirty axis is strong, but too many speakers and the narration all reach for the same exact wording. In the chapter's decisive exchange that shared vocabulary starts to compress Junie, Inez, Gideon, and Silas toward one authorial motif instead of letting each register express the conflict in its own grain.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the clean/dirty pair only at the most strategic setup/payoff points in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:31, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:45, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:91, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:251-253. Rephrase at least two of the middle or late echoes so Junie speaks in kitchen/service terms, Inez in maintenance or heat-control terms, Gideon in placement/accountancy terms, and narration in bodily consequence rather than repeating the motif label.

findings.acceptance_test: Across the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:91, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:173, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:231, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:245, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:275, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:321, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:327, no more than two sentence-initial evaluative "That..." constructions remain, and each touched paragraph ends on a specific sensory or social consequence rather than narrator labeling.
findings.evidence: Sentence-initial evaluative pivots recur at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:33 ("That was Junie"), snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:91 ("That was one of his nastier talents"), snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:173 ("That was the moment"), snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:231 ("That was the problem"), snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:245 ("That was part of the obscenity"), snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:275 ("That was another of the city's bad habits"), snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:321 ("That was the real victory"), and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:327 ("That too was human. That too was part of the problem").
findings.problem: The chapter leans too often on sentence-initial "That..." judgments to tell the reader what the preceding image means. Once or twice this is emphasis; at this density it becomes a prose tic that reduces variation and makes the narrator arrive with a label after the scene has already generated the inference.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the landings or turns at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:91, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:173, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:231, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:245, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:275, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:321, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:327 so more of those paragraphs land on concrete image, action, or bodily inference instead of an abstract summarizing clause.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if Inez's recognition at `snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5001` reads as uniquely this moment and no longer leans on 'went flatter' or adjacent still/flat shorthand. Fail if the revision keeps a generic flattening cue where the chapter needs a more singular wound.
findings.problem: Recurring full-book pattern: default emotional punctuation via 'went still/flat' appears in 5 chapters (chapters 03, 04, 07, 11, and 13). Here 'Her eyes went flatter' arrives at the betrayal pivot, and the recycled flattening beat slightly blurs what should feel like Inez's singular, devastating recognition.
findings.rewrite_direction: REPLACE the flattening formula at `snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5001` and tune the neighboring lines at `snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4999` and `snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5003` toward a reaction only this public betrayal could produce in Inez: a shift in grip, posture, voice, or a concrete decision already beginning inside her.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the book's strange civic biology, vulgar humor, erotic consequence, and class abrasion. When a choice appears between the cleaner version and the truer, messier one, choose the messier version so long as causality and character truth remain exact. Treat the city's slime, smell, heat, and appetite as part of the novel's beauty rather than embarrassment to be polished away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If sex, shame, hunger, class humiliation, bodily mess, or economic pressure starts drifting into decorous vagueness, rewrite until the scene carries concrete sensory and social cost on the page.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm across the novel; uncontracted forms should signal emphasis, class performance, ceremonial language, or Gideon's deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate crowd scenes with cleanup, ritual with labor, public humiliation with private tenderness, and single-sweep chapters with multi-scene accumulations; no two consecutive chapters should feel built from the same emotional weather, dialogue density, or scene rhythm.
prose_style_profile.diction: Period-aware but not museum-stiff; vivid, bodily, class-conscious, and willing to be ugly or funny without slipping into arch parody or modern snark.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored to Silas Vale for the full novel, with no head-hopping and no neutral historian voice; Inez remains legible through observed action, dialogue, and the objects she shares.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Elastic and pressure-sensitive: quick comic snap in public scenes, thicker sensory accumulation in labor and erotic passages, and hard clean sentences at moments of shame or decision.
